author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have loved, since the Lord will hear the voice of my prayer” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the soul that is sojourning in absence from the Lord sing thus, let that sheep which had strayed sing thus, let that son who had “died and returned to life,” who had “been lost and was found;” let our soul sing thus, brethren, and most beloved sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us be taught, and let us abide, and let us sing thus with the Saints: “I have loved: since the Lord will hear the voice of my prayer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this a reason for having loved, that the Lord will hear the voice of my prayer? and do we not rather love, because He hath heard, or that He may hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then meaneth, “I have loved, since the Lord will hear”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth he, because hope is wont to inflame love, say that he hath loved, since he hath hoped that God will listen to the voice of his prayer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence hath he hoped for this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since, he saith, “He hath inclined His ear unto me: and in my days I have called upon Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I loved, therefore, because He will hear; He will hear, “because He hath inclined His ear unto me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence knowest thou, O human soul, that God hath inclined His ear unto thee, except thou sayest, “I have believed”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These three things, therefore, “abide, faith, hope, charity:” because thou hast believed, thou hast hoped; because thou hast hoped, thou hast loved.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what are thy days, since thou hast said, “In my days I have called upon Him”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are they those perchance, in which “the fulness of time came,” and “God sent His Son,” who had already said, “In an acceptable time have I heard thee, and in a day of salvation have I helped thee”?…I may rather call my days the days of my misery, the days of my mortality, the days according to Adam, full of toil and sweat, the days according to the ancient corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I lying, stuck fast in the deep mire,” in another Psalm also have cried out, “Behold, Thou hast made my days old;” in these days of mine have I called upon Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For my days are different from the days of my Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I call those my days, which by my own daring I have made for myself, whereby I have forsaken Him: and, since He reigneth everywhere, and is all-powerful, and holdeth all things, I have deserved prison; that is, I have received the darkness of ignorance, and the bonds of mortality.…For in these days of mine, “The snares of death compassed me round about, and the pains of hell gat hold upon me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3): pains that would not have overtaken me, had I not wandered from Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now they have overtaken me; but I found them not, while I was rejoicing in the prosperity of the world, in which the snares of hell deceive the more.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But after “I too found trouble and heaviness, I called upon the Name of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For trouble and profitable sorrow I did not feel; trouble, wherein He giveth aid, unto whom it is said, “O be Thou our help in trouble: and vain is the help of man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I thought I might rejoice and exult in the vain help of man; but when I had heard from my Lord, “Blessed are they that mourn, for they shall be comforted:” I did not wait until I should lose those temporal blessings in which I rejoiced, and should then mourn: but I gave heed to that very misery of mine which caused me to rejoice in such things, which I both feared to lose, and yet could not retain; I gave heed to it firmly and courageously, and I saw that I was not only agonized by the adversities of this world, but even bound by its good fortune; and thus “I found the trouble and heaviness” which had escaped me, “and called upon the Name of the Lord; O Lord, I beseech Thee, deliver my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then the holy people of God say, “I called upon the Name of the Lord:” and let the remainder of the heathen hear, who do not as yet call upon the Name of the Lord; let them hear and seek, that they may discover trouble and heaviness, and may call upon the Name of the Lord, and be saved.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Gracious is the Lord, and righteous; yea, our God is merciful” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is gracious, righteous, and merciful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Gracious in the first place, because He hath inclined His ear unto me; and I knew not that the ear of God had approached my lips, till I was aroused by those beautiful feet, that I might call upon the Lord’s Name: for who hath called upon Him, save he whom He first called?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence therefore He is in the first place “gracious;” but “righteous,” because He scourgeth; and again, “merciful,” because He receiveth; for “He scourgeth every son whom He receiveth;” nor ought it to be so bitter to me that He scourgeth, as sweet that He receiveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how should not “The Lord, who keepeth little ones” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6), scourge those whom, when of mature age, He seeketh to be heirs; “for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I was in misery, and He helped me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He helped me, because I was in misery; for the pain which the physician causeth by his knife is not penal, but salutary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn again then unto thy rest, O my soul; for the Lord hath done good to thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7): not for thy deservings, or through thy strength; but because the Lord hath done good to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Since,” he saith, “He hath delivered my soul from death” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is wonderful, most beloved brethren, that, after he had said that his soul should turn unto rest, since the Lord had rewarded him; he added, since “He hath delivered my soul from death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did it turn unto rest, because it was delivered from death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not rest more usually said of death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the action of him whose life is rest, and death disquietude?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such then ought to be the action of the soul, as may tend to a quiet security, not one that may increase restless toil; since He hath delivered it from death, who, pitying it, said, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest,” etc. Meek therefore and humble, following, so to speak, Christ as its path, should the action of the soul be that tendeth towards repose; nevertheless, not slothful and supine; that it may finish its course, as it is written, “In quietness make perfect thy works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast delivered my soul from death, mine eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever feeleth the chain of this flesh, chanteth these things as fulfilled in hope towards himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is truly said, “I was in misery, and He delivered me;” but the Apostle saith this also truly, that we are saved by hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that we are delivered from death, is well said to be already fulfilled, so that we may understand the death of unbelievers, of whom he saith, “Leave the dead to bury their dead.”…He will then clear our eyes of tears, when He shall save our feet from falling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there will then be no slipping of our feet as they walk, when there will be no sliding of the weak flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now, however firm our path, which is Christ, be; yet since we place flesh, which we are enjoined to subdue, beneath us; in the very work of chastening and subduing it, it is a great thing not to fall: but not to slip in the flesh, who can attain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I shall please in the sight of the Lord, in the land of the living” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9).…We “labour” indeed now, because we are awaiting “the redemption of our body:” but, “when death shall have been swallowed up in victory, and this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal immortality;” then there will be no weeping, because there will be no falling; and no falling, because no corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore we shall then no longer labour to please, but we shall be entirely pleasing in the sight of the Lord, in the land of the living.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7. …“I believed,” saith he, “and therefore did I speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I was sorely brought down” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he suffered many tribulations, for the sake of the word which he faithfully held, faithfully preached; and he was sorely brought down; as they feared who loved the praise of men better than that of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what meaneth, “But I”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He should rather say, I believed, and therefore I have spoken, and I was sorely brought down: why did he add, “But I,” save because a man may be sorely brought down by those who oppose the truth, the truth itself cannot, which he believeth and speaketh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence also the Apostle, when he was speaking of his chain, saith, “the word of God is not bound.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So this man also, since there is one person of the holy witnesses, that is, of the Martyrs of God, saith, “I believed, and therefore will I speak.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I;” not that which I believed, not the word which I have delivered; “but I was sorely brought down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I said in my trance, All men are liars” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By trance he meaneth fear, which when persecutors threaten, and when the sufferings of torture or death impend, human weakness suffereth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this we understand, because in this Psalm the voice of Martyrs is heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For trance is used in another sense also, when the mind is not beside itself by fear, but is possessed by some inspiration of revelation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I said in my haste, All men are liars.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In consternation he hath had regard to his infirmity, and hath seen that he ought not to presume on himself; for as far as pertaineth to the man himself, he is a liar, but by the grace of God he is made true; lest yielding to the pressure of his enemies he might not speak what he had believed, but might deny it; even as it happened to Peter, since he had trusted in himself, and was to be taught that we ought not to trust in man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if every one ought not to trust in man, surely not in himself; because he is a man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rightly therefore in his fear did he perceive that every man was a liar; since they also whom no fear robs of their presence of mind, so that they never lie by yielding to the persecutors, are such by the gifts of God, not by their own strength.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What,” he asketh, “what reward shall I give unto the Lord, for all the benefits that He hath returned unto me?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith not, for all the benefits that He hath done unto me but “for all the benefits that He hath returned unto me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What deeds then on the man’s part had preceded, that all the benefits of God were not said to be given, but returned?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What had preceded, on the man’s part, save sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God therefore repayeth good for evil, whilst unto Him men repay evil for good; for such was the return of those who said, “This is the heir: come, let us kill him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this man seeketh what he may return unto the Lord, and findeth not, save out of those things which the Lord Himself returneth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will receive,” he saith, “the cup of salvation, and call upon the Name of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My vows will I render to the Lord, before all His people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath given thee the cup of salvation, which when thou takest, and callest upon the Name of the Lord, thou shalt return unto Him a reward for all that He hath returned unto thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who, save He who saith, “Are ye able to drink the cup that I shall drink of?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath given unto thee to imitate His sufferings, save He who hath suffered before for thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore, “Right dear in the sight of the Lord is the death of His Saints” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He purchased it by His Blood, which He first shed for the salvation of slaves, that they might not hesitate to shed their blood for the Lord’s Name; which, nevertheless, would be profitable for their own interests, not for those of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let therefore the slave purchased at so great a price confess his condition, and say, “Behold, O Lord, how that I am Thy servant: “I am Thy servant, and the son of Thine handmaid” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16).…This, therefore, is the son of the heavenly Jerusalem, which is above, the free mother of us all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And free indeed from sin she is, but the handmaid of righteousness; to whose sons still pilgrims it is said, “Ye have been called unto liberty;” and again he maketh them servants, when he saith, “but by love serve one another.”…Let therefore that servant say unto God, Many call themselves martyrs, many Thy servants, because they hold Thy Name in various heresies and errors; but since they are beside Thy Church, they are not the children of Thy handmaid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “I am Thy servant, and the son of Thine handmaid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast broken my bonds asunder.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will offer to Thee the sacrifice of praise” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I have not found any deserts of mine, since Thou hast broken my bonds asunder; I therefore owe Thee the sacrifice of praise; because, although I will boast that I am Thy servant, and the son of Thy handmaid, I will glory not in myself, but in Thee, my Lord, who hast broken asunder my bonds, that when I return from my desertion, I may again be bound unto Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will pay my vows unto the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What vows wilt thou pay?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What victims hast thou vowed? what burnt-offerings, what holocausts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou refer to what thou hast said a little before, “I will receive the cup of salvation, and will call upon the Name of the Lord;” and, “I will offer to Thee the sacrifice of thanksgiving”? and indeed whosoever well considereth what he is vowing to the Lord, and what vows he is paying, let him vow himself, let him pay himself as a vow: this is exacted, this is due.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On looking at the coin, the Lord saith, “Render unto Cæsar the things which are Cæsar’s, and unto God the things which are God’s:” his own image is rendered unto Cæsar: let His image be rendered unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the courts,” he saith, “of the Lord’s house” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the Lord’s house, the same is the Lord’s handmaid: and what is God’s house, save all His people?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It therefore followeth, “In the sight of all His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And now he more openly nameth his mother herself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what else is His people, but what followeth, “In the midst of thee, O Jerusalem”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For than that which is returned grateful, if it be returned from peace, and in peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who are not sons of this handmaid, have loved war rather than peace.…
